title: Understand: The Road to Reform, the Constitutional Convention, and the Threat of Mob Violence
description: Check your understanding on the road to reforming American government in this set of your multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: The Road to Reform, the Constitutional Convention, and the Battle for Ratification
The Road to Reform, the Constitutional Convention, and the Threat of Mob Violence
Understand: The Road to Reform, the Constitutional Convention, and the Threat of Mob Violence

choices.content: A revolt of farmers and war veterans who couldn’t pay their debts
A rebellion of Loyalists who wanted to return to British rule
The first battle of the Revolutionary War
An anti-slavery uprising

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to George Washington’s letter to Henry Knox, what would happen if the government in Massachusetts was unable to enforce its laws?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Anarchy and confusion
Peace and prosperity
Large payments to soldiers
Nothing of any importance

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was one cause of Shays’ Rebellion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Growing debt as a result of the Revolutionary War
Unrest caused by expansion into Native American territories
British seizure of colonial land after the Revolutionary War
Lack of  protection of  political rights after the ratification of the Constitution

assessment_items.question_content:
**In one of his letters to Henry Knox, what solution did George Washington propose to stop rebellions like Shays’ Rebellion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Revising the Articles of Confederation to create a stronger government
Encouraging Massachusetts delegates to revise their state constitution
Appealing to Great Britain to support the legitimacy of the American government
Improving the United States military to prevent any potential rebellions

assessment_items.question_content:
In a letter to Henry Knox, George Washington agreed that there needed to be a stronger national government. 

**What was the __main__ reason he cited for this change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The current government was too slow and too weak to deal with the problems of the nation.
There was too much power concentrated in the executive branch, which was not subject to the will of the people.
The states could not coordinate with each other to solve national problems like rebellions.
There were too many countries trying to take advantage of the United States, due to its inexperience as a nation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which national figure was NOT concerned about Shays’ Rebellion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Thomas Jefferson
George Washington
Alexander Hamilton
James Madison

assessment_items.question_content:
**What did George Washington and other national leaders call for in response to Shays’ Rebellion? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A convention to create a stronger federal government
A declaration of independence from the British monarchy
An end to the international slave trade
A bill of rights that could be added to a national constitution

assessment_items.question_content:
**Shays' Rebellion, an uprising of farmers and Revolutionary War veterans in Massachusetts, was a major cause of which event?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The creation of a stronger central government that would better be able to handle military and economic problems than the Articles of Confederation
The end of Native American sovereignty in western Massachusetts, resulting in the relocation of most Native Americans to regions west of the Mississippi River
The adoption of the Articles of Confederation, which gave the government the power to declare war against rebels
The declaration of war against the British monarchy, with the intention of ending colonial rule in the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the response of the Confederation Congress to Shays’ Rebellion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To pass strict laws punishing Massachusetts for the violence
To send George Washington’s American army to suppress the rebellion
To ratify an amendment securing Americans’ right to protest
To call for a convention to restructure the national government
A group motivated by passion that promotes change for their own benefit and not for the common good
A group that tries to implement political change through reason and deliberation
A group of religiously devoted people who keep to themselves and do not attempt to change government policies
A group that participates in politics in order to benefit all members of a society

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to James Madison in Federalist No. 10, what is one cause of factions in a constitutional republic?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Inflation that has gotten out of control
An executive with too much power
Unequal distribution of property and wealth
State governments without true power

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to Madison in Federalist No. 10, what is the best way to control factions?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Design a government that encourages groups to compete against each other
Encourage everyone to have the same opinions
Restrict individual liberties so that the people cannot influence the government
Ensure everyone has the same amount of wealth and property
